specific_concerns: Mapping 'that by which all this is pervaded' to 'POST /v1/payments returning 200 with this JSON shape' is a substantial register compression. A practitioner reading Shankara's bhasya — where 'all this is pervaded' refers to Brahman pervading the entire universe — could read this as collapsing Brahman into a JSON contract. The engineering layer mitigates by preserving the literal_meaning and naming the analog explicitly as a register translation, not an identity claim.
verdict_reason: Trivialization risk is real but managed. The engineering layer never claims the protocol IS Brahman; it claims the verse's grammatical structure (substrate / pervasion / imperishability under any single agent's authority) has an operational analog. The Sanskrit terms preserved are used as register anchors. The Hyrum's Law counter-example explicitly forecloses the trivialization (treating every accidental observable as 'imperishable substrate'). PASS at severity 2.
